expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: context intake preserves banding requirements, style constraints, and rendering assumptions before scenario drafting
expectations.evidence: In result.md: "What Phase 1 must preserve before scenario drafting (case focus)" followed by explicit sections "Banding functional requirements to preserve" (rows/columns, header-driven, color formatting), "Rendering / UX assumptions to preserve" (readability enhancement, Modern Grid in dashboards), and "Guardrail for this benchmark case" stating Phase 1 must keep these intact before scenario drafting.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase1
expectations.evidence: The output is framed as a Phase 1 contract check and avoids scenario drafting: title "Phase 1 (Context Intake) Contract Check"; execution_notes.md says "Kept the artifact aligned to Phase 1 (spawn/context intake), and did not draft scenarios." result.md also states it is "INCONCLUSIVE" due to missing "phase1_spawn_manifest.json", consistent with Phase 1 deliverable verification.
